---
title: "Session 06 teaching notes: Python 3"
session_id: session-06
event_id: null
---

## Session 6 — Statistics, charts, and grouping

ggf. Varianz/Mittelwert erkären

Plots: nur LIne/Bar in der Klausur (siehe Dokument: relevant topics for the exam)

-> ML/prediction: nicht Klausurrelevant

## Exercises

| Page(s) | Exercise                 | Summary                                                                                                      | Files                                                                     | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
|      42 | Descriptive statistics   | Calculate aggregates, summaries, correlations, distinct values, and continent frequencies.                   | `countries.csv`<br>`countries3.py`                                        |                      |
|      49 | Basic visualizations     | Create line, bar, pie, density, and box plots for the country data.                                          | `countries.csv`<br>`countries4.py`                                        |                      |
|      53 | Conditional filtering    | Filter countries using area, population, continent, and GDP conditions; summarize and visualize the results. | `countries.csv`<br>`countries5.py`                                        |                      |
|      56 | Grouping and aggregation | Use `groupby()` to summarize countries by continent and insurance contracts by state and coverage.           | `countries.csv`<br>`countries6.py`<br>`insurance.xlsx`<br>`insurance1.py` |                      |

::: {.teaching-break}
☕ Break — 10 minutes
:::

## Summary and announcements (next session)

TODO

<!--
    ## Before class

    - [ ] Review the session slides/materials.
    - [ ] Prepare files, datasets, and examples.
    - [ ] Note any announcements or reminders.

    ## Timing record

    Use this table while preparing or after teaching to record the actual timing.

    | Planned time | Actual time | Topic/activity | Notes for next time |
    |---|---|---|---|
    | 0--10 min |  | Setup / recap |  |
    | 10--30 min |  | Main input |  |
    | 30--60 min |  | Guided practice |  |
    | 60--80 min |  | Independent practice / questions |  |
    | 80--90 min |  | Wrap-up |  |

    ## Notes during class

    - TBD

    ## Follow-up

    - [ ] Update examples or instructions.
    - [ ] Record common questions.
    - [ ] Add reminders for the next session.
-->

## Introductory discussion

- Prior programming experience?
- Why go beyond Excel and use Python? (advanced analytics: Regression, prediction, ML, AI, ...)
- Why shouldn't we just use ChatGPT? (prompt adequately, validate and improve, Python: effective prompting possible)

- Advantage of Python: libraries! (PyPI)
- Interpreted: vs. compiled language
